data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: PASS / not-applicable.

T3 applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a capability it has not used before. This plan does neither — it (a) adds a project-level disable-the-gate config (fail-open), (b) documents a rollback/back-out path + deploy ordering, and (c) cross-references the epic spec. Every named mechanism is an INTERNAL, already-implemented-and-tested capability:
- The disable config mirrors the existing `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` / `require_signature_for_close` / `require_completion_verification_for_close` toggles (rebar.toml lines 5–21; rebar.toml:20 already states "Turning this off is the rollback").
- The HMAC signature scheme + `verify-signature` CLI + SIGNATURE/REVIEW_RESULT sidecar events are existing, tested internals (tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_signature.py, test_plan_review_gate.py; docs/plan-review-gate.md, docs/event-schema.md).

No external subcommand/endpoint, no new auth/HTTPS precondition, and no asserted-but-unverified external capability is introduced. Per the T3 ANTI-FP guidance ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"), this is a clean PASS with no findings.

Affirmations (T3): integration-as-described is achievable via existing internals; no capability gap; no SPIKE needed; auth/HTTPS not applicable (no external service).
